focus_points.label: How he’s landing with the team day-to-day.
focus_points.reason: In week two, he’s learned mostly via docs and async notes rather than direct contact. One possibility is he’s moving fast but missing the real operating texture—worth clarifying who he’s been working with, how often he’s getting live feedback, and what context he’s still not hearing.

focus_points.label: Ownership boundaries and what “good” means.
focus_points.reason: He may be building an internal map that differs from how the team actually operates. Whether his ownership boundaries and expectations are explicit enough for him to make safe calls without overstepping is worth clarifying early.

focus_points.label: Where he needs context before assumptions.
focus_points.reason: He’s at the stage where architecture assumptions can be made quickly from incomplete information. Ask what questions are still unanswered, which decisions feel risky, and where he’s currently waiting on clarity—so you can convert missing context into concrete onboarding support.

focus_points.label: Incident expectations and escalation paths.
focus_points.reason: Onboarding for a Mid backend engineer should include how incidents are handled in practice—what to monitor, when to escalate, and how to communicate hard news. Whether he knows the expectation for incident ownership and escalation early prevents “looks productive” drift into the wrong mental model.
